title: Observability, Service Levels, Support, and Incident Learning · Stakeholder Case: Service Level Indicator
summary: The product emits many logs but cannot answer whether users succeed, which revision failed, who is affected, or how to restore service. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp2 service level indicator increment in user-centered operations and incident system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries.
Maintain contribution, conduct, security, support, ownership, license, roadmap, release, and archival practices for project health.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
